references.title: Predicting Dropout in Higher Education Based on Secondary School Performance

extracted.Student Performance Definition: End of Program Graduation or Dropout
extracted.Courses: All courses in a Hungarian University
extracted.Data sources:
Enrollment data, 
Highschool Academic History, Student Characteristics
extracted.Features: [Student ID, Program ID, Year of Enrollment, Faculty of Program, Field of Program, Financial Status, High School ID, Name of Highschool, Type of Educational Institute, City and County, Admission points score, Study points, Matura exam points, Extra points, Base Points, Hungarian Language Matura Exam, MAths Matura Exam, History Matura Exam, Foreign Language Matura Exam, Language, Language Level, Consortium, Competition Achievement, APS calculating method, Gender, Data of Birth, Address]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors are required to adopt multiple pre-processing steps (imputation of nans, Feature Selection, etc…). Comparison of ddifferent Nan imputation strategies is performed.

Feature Engineering creates 4 families of variables: 
- Student ID
- Highschool-Related, 
- Highschool Performance Related,
- Personal details.
extracted.Models:
DT
Random Forest
Generalized Linear Model
Naive Bayes
AdaBoost
kNN
Logistic Regression
GBoost
Deep Learning
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {AUC: 0.811 - Deep Learning, Accuracy: 0.735 - Deep Learning, Recall: 0.897 - DT, Precision: 0.708 - Deep Learning}
extracted.Comments:
Authors do Early Prediction of Dropout by using only information that should be available at the time of admission of a learner.

Authors compare different forms of filling missing-values - Nans filled using kNN imputation outperformed the alternative preprocessing methods.

Prsopective studfents can provide their details and receive a prediction.
extracted.Limitations:
Only uses admission data.

A lot of the information is extremely personal, which may be a problem when it comes to ethics.

May potentially lead to some self-fulfilling prophecies where predicting dropout causes the dropout.

annotations.text: Predicting performance and Dropout are important research questions to address. Authors employ multiple algorithms to identify students at-risk and predict dropout. The model predicts end-of-program success  and follows a standard ML pipeline using 10-fold Cross-validation. Best models, evaluated using AUC score, were GBoost (0.808) and Deep Learning (0.811).
